bullets.text: Fed ends enforcement actions against ICBC and Standard Chartered, signaling compliance remediation and reduced regulatory overhang for global banks’ U.S. operations.
U.S. officials say Iran’s military is adapting as strikes intensify; unclear war timeline raises escalation and regional-spillover risks.
IEA countries weigh releasing oil reserves amid Hormuz disruption fears; potential supply boost could cap crude spikes and ease inflation pressures.
Iran conflict adds a portfolio ‘risk tax’—higher volatility and defense/cyber demand; investors rotate toward resilient cash flows and security vendors.
Oracle hits strongest earnings momentum in 15 years; AI-driven cloud growth (44%) lifts guidance, highlighting hyperscaler compute demand and new chip suppliers.
Amazon plans up to $42B bond sale, joining mega issuers; separately tightens controls after AI-related outages, requiring senior-engineer signoff on AI-assisted changes.
Sen. Tillis blocks Fed-chair nominee Kevin Warsh pending Powell probe; leadership uncertainty collides with ‘perfect storm’ of inflation, deficits, and geopolitical shocks.
Senate advances sweeping housing bill as home sales modestly rebound; sluggish supply growth keeps affordability tight, leaving rates and construction policy central.
Boaz Weinstein warns private credit ‘financial alchemy’ is worsening quarterly; opacity and leverage could amplify a downturn and force repricing in leveraged finance.
Meta buys Moltbook, a viral AI-bot social network; signals push toward agent ecosystems while raising integrity and misinformation risks from synthetic activity.
Whistleblower alleges DOGE-linked worker improperly accessed and exported Social Security data; incident underscores critical infrastructure cyber risk and potential regulatory fallout.
Federal appeals court ends SAVE student-loan repayment plan for millions; higher required payments may pressure household budgets and weigh on consumer spending.
Vertex’s povetacicept passes key IgA nephropathy trial test, strengthening pipeline for kidney disease and supporting biotech valuations in immunology.
Intel demonstrates fully homomorphic encryption chip enabling computation on encrypted data, potentially unlocking privacy-preserving cloud analytics and regulated AI workloads.
